verses.latin_vulgate: Audi igitur, Job, eloquia mea,  et omnes sermones meos ausculta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם שְׁמַֽע־נָ֣א אִיּ֣וֹב מִלָּ֑י וְֽכׇל־דְּבָרַ֥י הַאֲזִֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce aperui os meum :  loquatur lingua mea in faucibus meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־נָ֭א פָּתַ֣חְתִּי פִ֑י דִּבְּרָ֖ה לְשׁוֹנִ֣י בְחִכִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simplici corde meo sermones mei,  et sententiam puram labia mea loquentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹשֶׁר־לִבִּ֥י אֲמָרָ֑י וְדַ֥עַת שְׂ֝פָתַ֗י בָּר֥וּר מִלֵּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus Dei fecit me,  et spiraculum Omnipotentis vivificavit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֽוּחַ־אֵ֥ל עָשָׂ֑תְנִי וְנִשְׁמַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י תְּחַיֵּֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si potes, responde mihi,  et adversus faciem meam consiste.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תּוּכַ֥ל הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נִי עֶרְכָ֥הֿ לְ֝פָנַ֗י הִתְיַצָּֽבָה׃
verses.king_james: If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce, et me sicut et te fecit Deus,  et de eodem luto ego quoque formatus sum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵן־אֲנִ֣י כְפִ֣יךָ לָאֵ֑ל מֵ֝חֹ֗מֶר קֹרַ֥צְתִּי גַם־אָֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I am according to thy wish in God’s stead: I also am formed out of the clay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen miraculum meum non te terreat,  et eloquentia mea non sit tibi gravis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֣ה אֵ֭מָתִי לֹ֣א תְבַעֲתֶ֑ךָּ וְ֝אַכְפִּ֗י עָלֶ֥יךָ לֹֽא־יִכְבָּֽד׃
verses.king_james: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixisti ergo in auribus meis,  et vocem verborum tuorum audivi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭ךְ אָמַ֣רְתָּ בְאׇזְנָ֑י וְק֖וֹל מִלִּ֣ין אֶשְׁמָֽע׃
verses.king_james: Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Mundus sum ego, et absque delicto :  immaculatus, et non est iniquitas in me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זַ֥ךְ אֲנִ֗י בְּֽלִ֫י־פָ֥שַׁע חַ֥ף אָנֹכִ֑י וְלֹ֖א עָוֺ֣ן לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia querelas in me reperit,  ideo arbitratus est me inimicum sibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן תְּ֭נוּאוֹת עָלַ֣י יִמְצָ֑א יַחְשְׁבֵ֖נִי לְאוֹיֵ֣ב לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuit in nervo pedes meos ;  custodivit omnes semitas meas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָשֵׂ֣ם בַּסַּ֣ד רַגְלָ֑י יִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר כׇּל־אׇרְחֹתָֽי׃
verses.king_james: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc est ergo in quo non es justificatus :  respondebo tibi, quia major sit Deus homine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־זֹ֣את לֹא־צָדַ֣קְתָּ אֶעֱנֶ֑ךָּ כִּי־יִרְבֶּ֥ה אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ מֵאֱנֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adversus eum contendis,  quod non ad omnia verba responderit tibi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֭דּוּעַ אֵלָ֣יו רִיב֑וֹתָ כִּ֥י כׇל־דְּ֝בָרָ֗יו לֹ֣א יַעֲנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semel loquitur Deus,  et secundo idipsum non repetit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־בְאַחַ֥ת יְדַבֶּר־אֵ֑ל וּ֝בִשְׁתַּ֗יִם לֹ֣א יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per somnium, in visione nocturna,  quando irruit sopor super homines,  et dormiunt in lectulo,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּחֲל֤וֹם ׀ חֶזְי֬וֹן לַ֗יְלָה בִּנְפֹ֣ל תַּ֭רְדֵּמָה עַל־אֲנָשִׁ֑ים בִּ֝תְנוּמ֗וֹת עֲלֵ֣י מִשְׁכָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc aperit aures virorum,  et erudiens eos instruit disciplina,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ז יִ֭גְלֶה אֹ֣זֶן אֲנָשִׁ֑ים וּבְמֹ֖סָרָ֣ם יַחְתֹּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut avertat hominem ab his quæ facit,  et liberet eum de superbia,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְ֭הָסִיר אָדָ֣ם מַעֲשֶׂ֑ה וְגֵוָ֖ה מִגֶּ֣בֶר יְכַסֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.

verses.latin_vulgate: eruens animam ejus a corruptione,  et vitam illius ut non transeat in gladium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַחְשֹׂ֣ךְ נַ֭פְשׁוֹ מִנִּי־שָׁ֑חַת וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ מֵעֲבֹ֥ר בַּשָּֽׁלַח׃
verses.king_james: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Increpat quoque per dolorem in lectulo,  et omnia ossa ejus marcescere facit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהוּכַ֣ח בְּ֭מַכְאוֹב עַל־מִשְׁכָּב֑וֹ (וריב) [וְר֖וֹב] עֲצָמָ֣יו אֵתָֽן׃
verses.king_james: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominabilis ei fit in vita sua panis,  et animæ illius cibus ante desiderabilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְזִהֲמַ֣תּוּ חַיָּת֣וֹ לָ֑חֶם וְ֝נַפְשׁ֗וֹ מַאֲכַ֥ל תַּאֲוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabescet caro ejus,  et ossa, quæ tecta fuerant, nudabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֣כֶל בְּשָׂר֣וֹ מֵרֹ֑אִי (ושפי) [וְשֻׁפּ֥וּ] עַ֝צְמֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א רֻאּֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Appropinquavit corruptioni anima ejus,  et vita illius mortiferis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקְרַ֣ב לַשַּׁ֣חַת נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ לַֽמְמִתִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si fuerit pro eo angelus loquens,  unus de millibus, ut annuntiet hominis æquitatem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יֵ֤שׁ עָלָ֨יו ׀ מַלְאָ֗ךְ מֵלִ֗יץ אֶחָ֥ד מִנִּי־אָ֑לֶף לְהַגִּ֖יד לְאָדָ֣ם יׇשְׁרֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:

verses.latin_vulgate: miserebitur ejus, et dicet :  Libera eum, ut non descendat in corruptionem :  inveni in quo ei propitier.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחֻנֶּ֗נּוּ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר פְּ֭דָעֵהוּ מֵרֶ֥דֶת שָׁ֗חַת מָצָ֥אתִי כֹֽפֶר׃
verses.king_james: Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consumpta est caro ejus a suppliciis :  revertatur ad dies adolescentiæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֻֽטְפַ֣שׁ בְּשָׂר֣וֹ מִנֹּ֑עַר יָ֝שׁ֗וּב לִימֵ֥י עֲלוּמָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s: he shall return to the days of his youth:

verses.latin_vulgate: Deprecabitur Deum, et placabilis ei erit :  et videbit faciem ejus in jubilo,  et reddet homini justitiam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֶעְתַּ֤ר אֶל־אֱל֨וֹהַּ ׀ וַיִּרְצֵ֗הוּ וַיַּ֣רְא פָּ֭נָיו בִּתְרוּעָ֑ה וַיָּ֥שֶׁב לֶ֝אֱנ֗וֹשׁ צִדְקָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respiciet homines, et dicet : Peccavi,  et vere deliqui, et ut eram dignus, non recepi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָשֹׁ֤ר ׀ עַל־אֲנָשִׁ֗ים וַיֹּ֗אמֶר חָ֭טָאתִי וְיָשָׁ֥ר הֶעֱוֵ֗יתִי וְלֹא־שָׁ֥וָה לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;

verses.latin_vulgate: Liberavit animam suam, ne pergeret in interitum,  sed vivens lucem videret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּדָ֣ה (נפשי) [נַ֭פְשׁוֹ] מֵעֲבֹ֣ר בַּשָּׁ֑חַת (וחיתי) [וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ] בָּא֥וֹר תִּרְאֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce hæc omnia operatur Deus  tribus vicibus per singulos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־כׇּל־אֵ֭לֶּה יִפְעַל־אֵ֑ל פַּעֲמַ֖יִם שָׁל֣וֹשׁ עִם־גָּֽבֶר׃
verses.king_james: Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut revocet animas eorum a corruptione,  et illuminet luce viventium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְהָשִׁ֣יב נַ֭פְשׁוֹ מִנִּי־שָׁ֑חַת לֵ֝א֗וֹר בְּא֣וֹר הַחַיִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attende, Job, et audi me :  et tace, dum ego loquor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַקְשֵׁ֖ב אִיּ֥וֹב שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י הַ֝חֲרֵ֗שׁ וְאָנֹכִ֥י אֲדַבֵּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem habes quod loquaris, responde mihi :  loquere, volo enim te apparere justum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יֵשׁ־מִלִּ֥ין הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נִי דַּ֝בֵּ֗ר כִּֽי־חָפַ֥צְתִּי צַדְּקֶֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si non habes, audi me :  tace, et docebo te sapientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אַ֭יִן אַתָּ֥ה שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י הַ֝חֲרֵ֗שׁ וַאֲאַלֶּפְךָ֥ חׇכְמָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
